ongoing_stories.headline: China nationwide AV permit freeze
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active Day 40 as of 2026-06-08. No resumption timeline announced. Nikkei end-of-May deadline passed with no MIIT announcement. All three China-operating companies (Apollo Go, WeRide, Pony.ai) unable to expand fleet or enter new cities. MIIT/MOT sources network-restricted. Outage trigger date conflict previously RESOLVED: March 31 confirmed.
status_change_triggers: New permit issued to any operator

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan outage trigger date — RESOLVED 2026-06-08
ongoing_stories.last_status: RESOLVED 2026-06-08. March 31 2026 evening (~21:00 local) confirmed by Wuhan PSB Traffic Police April 1 government report, CnEVPost, TechNode, CNN, BusinessDay. April 27 date has zero credible source support — conflict cleared. Registry entry to be removed after 7-day retention window (remove after 2026-06-15).

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo NHTSA recall 3,791 vehicles flooding defect
ongoing_stories.last_status: Open Day 27 as of 2026-06-08. Interim constraints deployed OTA (flash-flood zone avoidance). Final software remedy still in development per NHTSA filing. Affected vehicles: all 5th- and 6th-gen ADS across Phoenix, SF, LA, Austin, San Antonio, Atlanta. No new update June 8.

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo freeway ride suspension construction zone edge cases
ongoing_stories.last_status: Ongoing Day 18 as of 2026-06-08. All freeway rides suspended in Bay Area, Los Angeles, Phoenix, and Miami. Surface street operations unaffected. Root cause: construction zone navigation edge cases. Trigger: viral May 21 incident (vehicle blasted through construction cones, pursued by police). No resumption date — Waymo says 'soon'. No new update June 8.

ongoing_stories.headline: Apollo Go Wuhan commercial service status post-outage
ongoing_stories.last_status: Status contested as of 2026-06-08 Day 69. Conflicting data: multiple Tier 2-3 sources suggest Wuhan commercial service has not resumed post-March 31 outage; one unverified source suggests partial resumption. Working conclusion: shut down (Score 4 vs Score 0). HIGH PRIORITY. conflict_age_days 11. ESCALATED per Task 3a.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: Automotive World / CNBC: Wuhan service shut down (Score 4). Anonymous tourist forum: partial service operational (Score 0). Working conclusion: shut down. HIGH PRIORITY escalated — conflict unresolved for 11 days.
status_change_triggers: Official Apollo Go / Baidu Wuhan resumption announcement

ongoing_stories.headline: Waymo Ojai US Senate scrutiny of Zeekr/Geely manufacturing partnership
ongoing_stories.last_status: Active as of 2026-06-08 Day 11 unchanged. No new Congressional action following February 2026 Senate Commerce hearing (Sen. Moreno accusations). No follow-up hearing scheduled. Waymo defense: Zeekr vehicles have no connectivity or smarts — Waymo installs ADS in US. Ojai deployed in US operations; London uses Jaguar I-Pace fleet. No new Senate statement or AV legislation movement detected June 8. conflict_age_days 11. HIGH PRIORITY ESCALATED.
ongoing_stories.conflicting_data: No confirmed follow-up Senate hearing date. Original hearing February 2026. conflict_age_days 11. HIGH PRIORITY.
status_change_triggers: USTR or Commerce formal review launched
Waymo formal response or supply-chain disclosure

ongoing_stories.headline: London robotaxi race: Waymo testing vs Uber+Wayve commercial launch plan
ongoing_stories.last_status: New as of 2026-06-08. Waymo testing 100 Jaguar I-Pace vehicles across 100-sq-mile London area (began April 2026, human safety operators). Uber and Wayve (UK-based AV startup) announced branded Ford Mustang Mach-E fleet, commercial service targeting coming months pending UK AV regulatory approval. UK government supportive but framework not finalized. No commercial launch date set for any party.
status_change_triggers: Commercial service launch by Waymo or Uber+Wayve
